key_insight: A fully automated OCT segmentation pipeline can precisely map RPE loss, EZ loss, and EZ thinning to monitor geographic atrophy progression in AMD.
clinical_relevance: This could improve real-world GA assessment by providing consistent, reproducible structural biomarkers for staging, follow-up, and treatment-response evaluation.
method: Three deep-learning semantic segmentation models identify RPE loss, EZ boundaries/interruptions, and Bruch’s membrane.
Trained on 298 diverse SD-OCT volumes spanning GA, intermediate AMD, neovascular AMD, and healthy eyes.
Validated on an independent external dataset and additional repeatability, reader-reliability, and scan-density analyses.
evidence_signal: The models showed high performance with Dice scores of 0.88 for RPE loss and 0.87 for EZ loss, Pearson’s r > 0.99, sub-pixel EZ thickness deviation of 2.15 µm, and ICC > 0.98.
watch_item: Performance may depend on OCT acquisition quality and B-scan density, so site-specific validation and workflow integration are important before broad deployment.
paper_title: Fully Automated High-Precision Segmentation of Retinal Atrophy and Ellipsoid Zone Thickness in OCT: A Reliable Tool for Real-World GA Monitoring
